# Implementation Plan: LifeSoundtrack — core command behavior (sandbox)

**Branch**: `001-lifesoundtrack-bot-commands` | **Date**: 2026-04-23 | **Spec**: [spec.md](spec.md)  
**Input**: Feature specification from `specs/001-lifesoundtrack-bot-commands/spec.md`  
**User constraints**: Build under **`bot/`**, **Go**, **minimal third-party libraries**; **platform-agnostic domain** per [spec.md](spec.md) **FR-007** and **Clarifications**.

**Note**: Generated by `/speckit-plan`. Phases 0–1 design are in this folder. **Telegram (or any single vendor)** appears only as the **first adapter**, not as a hard dependency of the product rules. Phase 2 tasks: [tasks.md](tasks.md).

## Summary

Deliver **LifeSoundtrack** **core command behavior** (start, help, ping, unknown → help hint) in **Go** under **`bot/`**, in a **transport-agnostic domain layer** that can be **reused** when swapping or adding a messaging platform (e.g. Telegram, WhatsApp, Discord). The **first** shipping adapter may use a specific Bot API and long polling, but the **plan and directory layout** treat that as a **replaceable** module behind a small **port** (interfaces). **No database**; configuration via environment (e.g. token for the active adapter). Add **`bot/Dockerfile`** and **Compose** for local run per constitution **VIII**; **structured logging**; no token leakage.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: Go **1.22+** (align with `.cursor/rules/go-api-standard-library-1-22.mdc` where applicable)  
**Domain layer**: **No** third-party chat SDKs—pure Go in `bot/internal/core/` (or `internal/domain/`): command routing, string copy, and tests over **plain types** (e.g. "which domain command" + "reply text").

**Adapters (platform-specific)**: **One** third-party (or hand-rolled) client **per platform** in `bot/internal/adapter/<name>/` (e.g. `adapter/telegram` using `github.com/go-telegram/bot` for the first milestone — see [research.md](research.md)). New platforms add a new `adapter/*` and wire it in `cmd/bot` without forking **FR** rules.

**Primary Dependencies (MVP)**: In the **adapter** for the first platform, **at most one** client module as in [research.md](research.md); **stdlib** elsewhere (`log/slog`, `context`, `os`, `os/signal`, etc.).

**Storage**: **N/A** (no persistence in spec v1)  
**Testing**: `go test ./...` from `bot/`; **table tests on `internal/core` without network**; adapter tests with fakes or minimal integration; manual sandbox per [quickstart.md](quickstart.md) for the **chosen** adapter.  
**Target Platform**: Linux (container) and local developer machines (Windows/macOS/Linux)  
**Project Type**: Long-running process; **receive mode** (long polling vs webhook) is an **adapter** concern; default for first Telegram-like adapter: **long polling** in sandbox.  
**Performance Goals**: **N/A** (spec)  
**Constraints**: **Private 1:1**; credentials via env; **never** log secrets; English-only user-facing strings; domain copy **identical** across adapters for a given build unless spec is amended.  
**Scale/Scope**: Single process, single product identity; no multi-tenant in v1

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Satisfied.*

Confirm alignment with [`.specify/memory/constitution.md`](../../.specify/memory/constitution.md):

- **Code quality**: `gofmt`, `go vet`, optional `golangci-lint` under `bot/`
- **REST API**: **N/A** (not a public HTTP product; optional webhook in an adapter is not a public REST API in this spec)
- **Testing**: Unit tests for **core**; manual sandbox for active adapter; CI runs `go test` when available
- **User experience**: Centralized **domain** copy; mapping to [contracts/messaging-commands.md](contracts/messaging-commands.md)
- **Monitoring / Logging / Performance / Containerization / Documentation accuracy**: as before; plan structure updated for **IX** (README with real `bot/` + adapters)

*Post–Phase 1: Domain/adapter split adds no forbidden REST product; **pass**.*

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
specs/001-lifesoundtrack-bot-commands/
├── spec.md
├── plan.md
├── research.md
├── data-model.md
├── quickstart.md
├── tasks.md
├── checklists/
│   └── requirements.md
└── contracts/
    └── messaging-commands.md
```

### Source Code (repository root) — **domain + adapters**

```text
bot/
├── go.mod
├── go.sum
├── cmd/
│   └── bot/
│       └── main.go            # select adapter, wire core
├── internal/
│   ├── core/                  # domain: Command enum, reply text, unknown hint (FR-001–FR-007)
│   ├── adapter/
│   │   └── telegram/         # first milestone: long poll, map updates → core
│   │       # (future) whatsapp/ discord/ ...
│   ├── config/               # env: token, adapter name, log level
│   └── (optional) ports.go   # interfaces: IncomingMessage, OutgoingReply, BotTransport
├── internal/core/..._test.go
├── Dockerfile
└── .env.example
```

**Structure Decision**: **Hexagonal / ports-and-adapters** — `internal/core` has **no** imports of Telegram (or any vendor). `internal/adapter/telegram` depends on `core` and the **Telegram** library only. `cmd/bot` composes one adapter. This satisfies **FR-007** and the clarification session.

## Phase 0 & Phase 1 Artifacts

| Artifact | Path | Purpose |
|----------|------|---------|
| Research | [research.md](research.md) | First adapter library, polling vs webhook, **port** boundaries |
| Data model | [data-model.md](data-model.md) | No DB; **domain** types for commands |
| Contracts | [contracts/messaging-commands.md](contracts/messaging-commands.md) | Platform-neutral **behavioral** contract |
| Quickstart | [quickstart.md](quickstart.md) | Sandbox runbook (defaults to first adapter) |

## Complexity Tracking

| Violation | Why Needed | Simpler Alternative Rejected Because |
|-----------|------------|-------------------------------------|
| **Two-layer structure** (core + adapter) vs a single `handlers` file | **FR-007** and clarification require reusable domain; single-file all-in-one would entangle copy with Telegram types | N/A — required by spec |
